message:
fix(cloudflare): percent-encode KV keys in URL paths + filter invalid HTTP status codes

KV keys can legitimately contain `/` and other special characters, but
they must be percent-encoded when placed into the URL path to prevent
the HTTP server from splitting them into multiple path segments.

Changed sanitize_kv_key() -> encode_kv_key() which validates AND
percent-encodes the key using NON_ALPHANUMERIC encoding set.

Also filtered Api error code -> status_code mapping to only include
valid HTTP status codes (100-599), not Cloudflare-specific error codes
(e.g. 6003, 10000) which would be semantically wrong in FcpError::External.

52 tests passing, clippy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
